'''Rob Butler''' (born 15 May 1998) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Betfred Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|accessdate=24 October 2019|archive-date=25 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191025220458/https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|date=20 October 2019|publisher=WWOS|title=England Knights v Jamaica|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|url-status=live}}</ref>

He has previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Warrington Wolves]] the [[Super League]], and has spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[London Skolars]] and the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

==Background==
Butler was born in [[Rochester, Kent|Rochester]], Kent, England and was a player at Medway Dragons RLFC for nine years as a junior.

==Career==
===London Broncos===
In 2018 Butler made his professional debut for the [[London Broncos]] against the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in Round 4 of the [[Betfred Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Cawdell|first=Luke|date=2018-03-08|title=Robert Butler made his senior debut for the London Broncos against Toronto Wolfpack|url=https://www.kentonline.co.uk/medway/sport/robert-butler-london-broncos-debut-medway-dragons-161197/|url-status=live|access-date=2022-01-01|website=Kent Online|language=en|archive-date=2022-01-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220101180213/https://www.kentonline.co.uk/medway/sport/robert-butler-london-broncos-debut-medway-dragons-161197/}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
On 24 November 2020 it was announced that Butler had signed for the [[Warrington Wolves]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2020/november/Rising-star-Rob-Butler-joins-the-Wire/|title=RISING STAR ROB BUTLER JOINS THE WIRE|website=www.warringtonwolves.com|accessdate=17 Dec 2020|archive-date=24 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201124133031/https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2020/november/Rising-star-Rob-Butler-joins-the-Wire/|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (loan)===
On 8 July 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]] on loan. Butler would later sign a contract to re-join the London Broncos.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Jul 2021|accessdate=9 Jul 2021|title=Leigh sign Warrington prop Rob Butler on loan|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-sign-warrington-prop-rob-butler-on-loan/|archive-date=8 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210708151323/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-sign-warrington-prop-rob-butler-on-loan/|url-status=live}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 15 October 2023, Butler played in the London Broncos upset [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
On 2 Oct 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=2 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=London Broncos forward leaves club for new challenge|url=https://www.totalrl.com/london-broncos-forward-leaves-club-for-new-challenge/}}</ref>

==International career==
He was called up to the England Knights training squad in July 2019.<ref>{{cite web | access-date=2019-11-22 | archive-date=2019-11-17 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191117194345/https://www.rugby-league.com/article/55858/teenage-trio-called-up-by-england-knights | url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/55858/teenage-trio-called-up-by-england-knights | title=Teenage trio called up by England Knights | url-status=live}}</ref>

In 2019 he made his international debut for the [[England Knights]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019|archive-date=1 November 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191101225926/https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|url-status=live}}</ref>
